title: Bucket Types, Endpoints, Regions, and S3 Express · Go Semantics Case: Directory Session Reactivation
summary: A client hard-codes path-style URLs, assumes global ordering and lifecycle features, and deploys latency-sensitive work without selecting a compatible bucket type or zone. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the directory session reactivation increment in bucket-type and endpoint compatibility matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Model directory-bucket session authorization, inactivity, 503 reactivation, retry boundaries, and unsupported lifecycle or versioning.
Resolve intended region and endpoint explicitly and reject redirects, signing-region drift, and accidental cross-region transfer.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Directory Session Reactivation · Theory Directory Session Reactivati…
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 7184 during a cost-anomaly investigation. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate general or directory bucket region, zone, endpoint, reactivation, and session rules; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then introduce a duplicate event or ambiguous response and reconcile without duplicate effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
